sections.paragraph:
αὐλίζομαι impf. ηὐλιζόμην. Pass.: fut. αὐλισθήσομαι LXX; JosAs 9:5; 1 aor. ηὐλίσθην (s. αὐλή; Hom.+; OGI 730, 7 [III b.c.]; pap; LXX; JosAs 9:4f; Joseph.) orig. of spending the night in the αὐλή, viz. in the open air, then also of temporary lodging, w. context indicating whether outdoors (‘bivouac’) or indoors.

① gener. to have a temporary sleeping arrangement, spend the night (Eupolis [V b.c.] 322; Nicol. Dam.: 90 Fgm. 4 p. 332, 17 Jac. [indoors]; Arrian, Anab. 6, 25, 5 [army bivouacking]; Ctesias: 688 Fgm. 24 Jac. [=Ps.-Demetr., Eloc. §216] ‘where noble men must spend the night’—in euphem. ref. to the death of Cyrus as sleep; Judg 19:6f, 10f; 20:4B; Ruth 3:13 αὐλίσθητι τὴν νύκτα al. LXX; Jos., Ant. 1, 279) ἐκεῖ (Judg 19:7; Tob 6:1BA) Mt 21:17; εἴς τι Lk 21:37 (cp. Tob 14:9BA μηκέτι αὐλισθῆτε εἰς Νινευή, but the sense here is ‘stay’, s. 2 below; spend some time is also poss. for the Lk pass., but bivouac is certainly prob. in view of the chosen locale, s. 22:39). ἕως οὗ αὐλισθῇ until he finds lodging (again) D 11:6 (indoors).

② to have housing, without special ref. to nighttime, live, stay (Epict., Gnomolog., Fgm. 47; Himerius, Or. 54 [15], 1 αὐλίζ. ἡμέραν μίαν, accordingly not at night; of stabling animals BASP 24/1–2, ’87, p. 10, lines 17f [PCol inv. 316] α̣ὐ̣[λι]ζέσθω; Sir 51:23; Jos., Bell. 1, 334; 2, 301) 1 Cl 4:11.—DELG s.v. αὐλή. M-M.

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

aor. aor. = aorist

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

orig. orig. = original(ly)

viz. viz. = videlicet (namely)

gener. gener. = generally

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

Ctesias Ctesias , V–IVb.c.—List 5

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

euphem. euphem. = euphemism

ref. ref. = reference(s)

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

BA BA = Biblical Archaeologist—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

poss. poss. = possible

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

prob. prob. = probable, probably

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Himerius Himerius , IV a.d.—List 5

BASP BASP = Bulletin of the American Society of Papyrologists—Lists 4, 6

inv. inv. = inventory (usually of numbers of papyri as inventoried)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 150
